pages.title: Creating Your First Datapack
pages.text: To create mob leveling rules, you need a datapack. Here's the minimum structure:$(br2)$osaves/<world>/datapacks/<pack_name>/$r$(br2)$opack.mcmeta$r (metadata file)$(br2)$odata/<namespace>/mob_levels/<rule_type>/<file>.json$

pages.title: Pack Metadata
pages.text: Your $(o)pack.mcmeta$r should look like this:$(br2){$(br)  "pack": {$(br)    "description": "My Mob Leveling Rules",$(br)    "pack_format": 15$(br)  }$(br)}$(br2)Pack format 15 is for Minecraft 1.20.1. Use 15+ for this version.

pages.title: Namespace
pages.text: Replace $(o)<namespace>$r with your modpack or datapack ID:$(br2)$(li)Use only lowercase letters, numbers, and underscores$(li)Keep it short but unique$(li)Examples: $(o)mypack, custom_rpg, hardcore_plus$$(br2)All your rules will be under:$(br2)$odata/<namespace>/mob_levels/$

pages.title: Rule Type Folders
pages.text: Create folders for each rule type:$(br2)$(li)$(bold)structures/$() - Structure-based rules$(li)$(bold)biomes/$() - Biome-based rules$(li)$(bold)base/$() - Base rules by mob type$(li)$(bold)bosses/$() - Boss definitions$(br2)The folder name determines the rule type. Files inside are automatically routed based on this path.
